---
title: "Salmon data package + SPSR intake path"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
---

Use this page when your goal is to move from **mapped data** to a **submission-ready salmon data package** and a route-scoped SPSR upload.

**Canonical package specification (Markdown):** <https://github.com/dfo-pacific-science/smn-data-pkg/blob/main/SPECIFICATION.md>

```{=html}
<div class="status-badge-row">
  <span class="status-badge status-active">Operational Intake Path</span>
  <span class="status-badge status-canonical">Package-first</span>
  <span class="status-badge status-guidance">Route-scoped</span>
</div>
```

::: {.callout-note}
This page is for **packaging and intake**.

If you need to propose new vocabulary terms or ontology changes, use [Controlled Vocabulary Governance Workflow](../../tutorials/support_controlled_vocabs.qmd).
:::

## Quick summary

- Start with a **salmon data package (SDP)** as the source of truth.
- The canonical package layout is **`metadata/` + `data/`**, with an optional root `datapackage.json`.
- **metasalmon** is the validation gate before submission.
- SPSR wizard uploads and bulk uploads should converge on the **same package-first path**.
- Current minimum route-scoped support story includes **CU/composite**, **SMU**, and **Population** routes.
- Guided assistants can help draft mappings and metadata, but validation remains the quality gate.

## Practical workflow

1. Find canonical terms in [GC DFO Salmon Ontology documentation](../ontology/formal-documentation.qmd).
2. Build `metadata/column_dictionary.csv` with full IRIs (no shortened or partial URIs).
3. Assemble the package using the [Salmon data package specification](salmon-data-exchange-package.qmd).
4. Validate locally with **metasalmon** or equivalent checks.
5. Choose the SPSR route scope that matches your data grain:
   - **CU/composite**
   - **SMU**
   - **Population**
6. Generate or align route-scoped upload CSV(s) from the same package using the current SPSR templates.
7. Upload through SPSR and review validation feedback.

For bulk uploads, use the same package-first flow. Do **not** maintain a separate spreadsheet-only bulk route that drifts away from the package metadata.

## Canonical package layout

Use this structure unless a destination workflow explicitly requires something else:

```text
my-salmon-data-package/
├── datapackage.json          # optional
├── metadata/
│   ├── dataset.csv
│   ├── tables.csv
│   ├── column_dictionary.csv
│   └── codes.csv             # optional
└── data/
    ├── table_1.csv
    └── table_2.csv
```

## SPSR touchpoints

- App documentation: <https://spsr.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/documentation/>
- Upload wizard: <https://spsr.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/wizard/1/>
- Template package endpoint: <https://spsr.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/download_sdp_templates>
- Repo: <https://github.com/dfo-pacific-science/salmon-population-summary-repository>

## Recommended artifacts

- `metadata/dataset.csv` for dataset-level metadata
- `metadata/tables.csv` for table inventory and entities
- `metadata/column_dictionary.csv` for column semantics and term links
- `metadata/codes.csv` for controlled value sets when needed
- `data/*.csv` for route-aligned source tables
- optional root `datapackage.json`
- route-scoped upload CSV(s) derived from the same package

## Validation gates

Before upload, confirm:

- all required metadata fields are present
- all semantic links use canonical full IRIs
- categorical values are mapped to controlled concepts
- provenance notes are present for major transformations
- the selected SPSR route scope matches the grain of the package data
- bulk upload files and wizard upload files still trace back to the same package version

## Cookbook + examples

- [CU/composite escapement estimates for package-first SPSR intake](../../cookbook/cu-composite-escapement-estimates.qmd)
- [FSAR Data Standardization Workflow](../../how_to_guides/fsar-data-standardization-workflow.qmd)
- [FSAR to SPSR: End-to-End Example](../../how_to_guides/fsar-to-spsr-end-to-end-example.qmd)

## References

- [Semantic Salmon Data Ecosystem](semantic-salmon-data-ecosystem.qmd)
- [Salmon data package specification](salmon-data-exchange-package.qmd)
- [Controlled Vocabulary Governance Workflow](../../tutorials/support_controlled_vocabs.qmd)
